tasks.description: In src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx: add `isInPinnedShotGroup?: boolean` to ClipActionProps (~lines 18-50), include it in the Pick for ClipContextMenuProps (~line 56), and in areClipActionPropsEqual (~line 452). Thread it through ClipActionComponent destructure and pass into <ClipContextMenu> JSX (~lines 421-446). Inside ClipContextMenu render (~lines 160-232), wrap divider + Split Here + batch Mute + Split-at-playhead + shot-actions block + Delete sections in `{!props.isInPinnedShotGroup && (...)}`. Leave the asset-state items at lines 147-159 (Update to current variant / Dismiss reminder) intact, but suppress the trailing divider when nothing follows. In handleContextMenu (~line 310), if isInPinnedShotGroup AND neither (isGenerationAsset && onUpdateVariant) nor (isVariantStale && onDismissStale) holds, early-return after preventDefault/stopPropagation. Apply the same early-return to the amber stale-badge onClick (~lines 410-414).

In src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx getActionRender (~line 542), pass `isInPinnedShotGroup={shotGroupClipIds.has(action.id)}` to the rendered <ClipAction>. shotGroupClipIds already exists (~line 321) and is already in the deps array (~line 598).

Extend src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts so a single resize can carry an adjacent clip. (a) Widen onActionResizeStart (~lines 26, 69-89) to accept optional `adjacent?: { action: TimelineAction; row: TimelineRow; dir: ResizeDir }`. Generate a single `txnId = crypto.randomUUID()` and assign it to `resizeTransactionIdRef.current[action.id]` AND, when adjacent is set, to `resizeTransactionIdRef.current[adjacent.action.id]`. Populate `resizeStartRef.current[adjacent.action.id]` with the same origin shape (from/to/speed) used for the primary, sourced from current committed clip state. (b) Widen onActionResizeEnd (~lines 27, 91-199) to accept optional `adjacent?: { action; row; start; end; dir }`. Refactor the meta-math/overlap-resolution body into a helper `computePerClipUpdate(action, row, dir, clipMeta, origin)` returning `{ rowsAfter, metaUpdates }`. In the end callback: apply primary first in-memory, carry forward to adjacent's input rows, apply adjacent on top, then issue ONE applyEdit with merged rows + merged metaUpdates using the primary's transactionId. Clean up both resizeStartRef and resizeTransactionIdRef entries for both ids. Existing single-clip call sites must keep compiling unchanged.

Widen the type of onActionResizeStart/End through every consumer so the optional `adjacent` field reaches TimelineCanvas: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts (~line 85), src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.contexts.ts (~lines 139, 205), src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorProvider.tsx (~line 107), and src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx (~line 170). Type-only widening; existing call sites with no adjacent should still compile.

Wire auto-shift in src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx. (a) In handleResizePointerDown (~line 674): find the shotGroups entry where rowId===row.id and clipIds.includes(action.id); identify prev/next neighbor by index; pick neighbor based on dir (left⇒prev, right⇒next); free outer edge ⇒ no neighbor. Extend ResizeSession with `adjacent?: { actionId, rowId, initialStart, initialEnd, dir }` (neighbor's dir is opposite of primary's). Call onActionResizeStart with `{ action, row, dir, adjacent }`. (b) In updateResize (~line 578): after computing primary start/end and BEFORE writing overrides, if session.adjacent: for dir==='left', adjEnd=start, adjStart=session.adjacent.initialStart, enforce adjEnd-adjStart >= minDuration by clamping primary `start` upward to adjStart+minDuration and recomputing adjEnd; mirror for dir==='right'. SKIP the existing snapResize call (~line 591) when session.adjacent is set (the neighbor IS the snap target). Free outer edges keep snapping. Write both overrides in one setResizeOverrides call and update resizePreviewRef.current for both ids. (c) In endResize (~line 647): read final overrides for both clips; call onActionResizeEnd ONCE with `{ action, row, start, end, dir, adjacent: { ... } }`; clearResizeOverride for both ids. Add shotGroups to deps as needed (already a prop used at ~line 430).

Update src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx with the new gating cases: (1) isInPinnedShotGroup=true + no asset-state actions ⇒ right-click does NOT mount the menu; (2) isInPinnedShotGroup=true + isVariantStale + onDismissStale ⇒ menu opens, contains 'Dismiss reminder' (and 'Update to current variant' if applicable), does NOT contain Split Here, Delete Clip, Create Shot, Generate Video, existing-shots list; (3) isInPinnedShotGroup=true ⇒ amber stale-badge click is a no-op when no asset-state actions are available; (4) ensure existing isInPinnedShotGroup=false / unspecified cases continue to pass unchanged.

Update src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx with a 3-clip pinned shotGroups resize test: (a) drag middle clip's left handle inward by N px ⇒ onActionResizeStart and onActionResizeEnd each called ONCE with `adjacent` populated for the previous clip; assert primary.start === adjacent.end, adjacent.dir==='right', primary.dir==='left'; (b) drag first clip's left handle outward ⇒ each callback called once with `adjacent: undefined`; (c) drag past minDuration on the neighbor ⇒ primary.start clamped (assert via the values passed to onActionResizeEnd). Leave the unrelated pre-existing data-shot-group-kind='pinned' failure alone.

Run focused tests then full suite. Commands: `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` then `npx vitest run`. Also run `npx tsc --noEmit` (or rely on vitest transpile) to confirm the type widening compiles cleanly across all consumers. If anything fails (other than the known pre-existing data-shot-group-kind='pinned' failure), read the error, fix the code, and re-run until green. Do NOT create new test files. Then write a short throwaway script (or manual repro recipe captured in chat) that exercises: pin a 3-clip shot group, drag the middle clip's left edge inward, confirm the adjacent clip's right edge follows and the group label still renders; right-click each clip and confirm only asset-state actions appear; verify single undo restores both edges. Delete the throwaway script when done.

watch_items: FLAG-001 (addressed): the resize commit MUST go through one applyEdit with one shared transactionId, and BOTH clips' origins (from/to/speed) MUST be recorded in resizeStartRef at onActionResizeStart time. Calling onActionResizeEnd twice from TimelineCanvas is wrong — do not regress to that shape.

FLAG-002 (open, minor): TimelineCanvas tests only assert callback payloads at the canvas boundary; they will NOT catch a broken adjacent-clip commit path inside useClipResize. Consider adding at least one assertion that exercises useClipResize's onActionResizeEnd directly (or via a higher-level integration test) so a wrong meta-math change is caught.
resolveOverlaps invoked sequentially on rows for primary then adjacent must produce stable results — if not, compose row updates first then run a single overlap pass.
shotGroups prop assumed to list clips in left-to-right order within each group (matches positionedShotGroups iteration). Verify before relying on prev/next index logic.
minDuration clamp must clamp the PRIMARY drag, not collapse the neighbor.
Skip snapResize ONLY when session.adjacent is set (interior group edge). Free outer edges (first-clip left, last-clip right) must keep existing snap behavior.
Type widening must reach useTimelineState.types.ts, useTimelineState.contexts.ts, VideoEditorProvider.tsx, TimelineEditor.tsx — missing one will break the build.
Apply early-return gating to BOTH handleContextMenu and the amber stale-badge onClick for consistency.
Suppress trailing divider in ClipContextMenu when the gated section is hidden so the menu doesn't render with a stray separator.
Pre-existing data-shot-group-kind='pinned' test failure is unrelated — leave it alone per the brief.
Scope guard: do NOT touch files outside src/tools/video-editor/. No DB/migrations/edge functions, no new toasts, no hardcoded colors.
Single-undo-step is a MUST criterion: verify manually that one resize action produces exactly one undo entry.

sense_checks.question: Does ClipContextMenu hide Split Here / Delete / Mute / Create Shot / shots list / Generate Video when isInPinnedShotGroup=true while still showing Update-to-current-variant and Dismiss-reminder when applicable, and does handleContextMenu (and the amber badge onClick) early-return when no asset-state action is available?
Is isInPinnedShotGroup={shotGroupClipIds.has(action.id)} actually being passed in getActionRender, and does shotGroupClipIds remain in the memo deps array?

Does onActionResizeStart record both clips' origins (from/to/speed) under one shared transactionId, and does onActionResizeEnd issue exactly ONE applyEdit with merged rows + merged metaUpdates regardless of whether adjacent is present?
Does the optional adjacent type flow cleanly through useTimelineState.types.ts, useTimelineState.contexts.ts, VideoEditorProvider.tsx, and TimelineEditor.tsx without breaking any existing call site?

Does TimelineCanvas resolve the neighbor at pointerDown, write both overrides during drag, skip snapResize only on interior group edges, clamp the primary drag at neighbor minDuration, and call onActionResizeEnd exactly once with the adjacent payload?
Do the new ClipAction.test.tsx cases cover (a) gated no-op, (b) gated menu with stale reminder, (c) gated stale-badge no-op, while existing non-grouped tests still pass?

Does the TimelineCanvas resize test cover interior left drag with adjacent populated, free-edge drag with adjacent undefined, and minDuration clamp on the neighbor — all asserting one call each to start/end?
Do the focused vitest runs and the full suite pass (excluding the known pre-existing pinned-data-attribute failure), and does manual smoke confirm gating + neighbor follow + single undo step?

meta_commentary: Two-change scope. Change 1 (gating) is mechanical and independent — start with T1+T2+T6 to land it cleanly, since they don't touch the resize pipeline. Change 2 (auto-shift) is the riskier half: the FLAG-001 fix requires extending useClipResize's onActionResizeStart/End signature so a single resize carries an adjacent clip under ONE shared transactionId, with BOTH clips' from/to/speed origins recorded at start time. Do NOT shortcut this by calling onActionResizeEnd twice from TimelineCanvas — that breaks meta math (neighbor would have no recorded origin) and splits the undo step. The end callback must issue exactly ONE applyEdit. Type-widening (T4) needs to reach all 5 consumer files in the video-editor folder; missing one breaks the build. In TimelineCanvas, the neighbor's dir is the OPPOSITE of the primary's (left drag on middle clip ⇒ adjacent is prev clip, adjacent.dir='right'). minDuration clamp clamps the PRIMARY drag, not the neighbor. Skip snapResize ONLY when session.adjacent is set — free outer edges (first-clip left, last-clip right) must keep snapping. FLAG-002 is open-minor: tests only spy on canvas-boundary callbacks, so a broken useClipResize commit path could slip through; the executor should verify manually with the smoke test in T8 that one resize = one undo step and that meta is correct after undo/redo. Pre-existing data-shot-group-kind='pinned' test failure is unrelated — do not touch it.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Gate per-clip context menu in ClipAction.tsx (props, threading, render gating, handleContextMenu + stale-badge early-return)
Step 3: Extend useClipResize start/end to accept optional adjacent clip with shared txnId and single applyEdit
Step 4: TimelineCanvas wiring — resolve neighbor, dual overrides during drag, single end callback, skip snap on interior edges
Step 5: Tests — ClipAction gating cases and TimelineCanvas resize auto-shift cases; run focused then full suite

validation.completeness_notes: All 5 plan steps mapped. T4 is broken out from Step 3 because the type widening reaching all consumers is itself a 'must' success criterion and is the most likely build-break point — separating it makes the dependency on T5 explicit. T8 covers Step 5's focused + full suite runs and the validation order's type-check + manual smoke.
